entries.title: US strikes Iranian fast boats as Iran attacks UAE oil facility
entries.snippet: Shipping company Maersk says one of its US-flagged commercial vessels has successfully exited the Strait of Hormuz under US military protection.

entries.title: Two killed and many injured after car driven into crowd in German city of Leipzig
entries.snippet: A 33-year-old German citizen was detained following the incident, the authorities say.

entries.title: Three dead in suspected hantavirus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
entries.snippet: The operator of the MV Hondius ship says a Dutch husband and wife, as well as a German national, have died but the cause has not yet been established.

entries.title: Former New York City mayor Rudy Giuliani recovering from pneumonia and 'now breathing on his own'
entries.snippet: The ex-New York mayor 'remains in critical but stable condition', according to his spokesman.

entries.title: Ukrainian drone hits upmarket Moscow high-rise ahead of Victory Day celebrations
entries.snippet: Russia has declared a unilateral ceasefire to cover the event and threatened a massive strike on central Kyiv if it were disrupted.

entries.title: Britney Spears pleads guilty to reckless driving after arrest
entries.snippet: The 44-year-old will avoid jail time after her arrest in California earlier this year.

entries.title: Three Russian diplomats expelled from Austria over spying accusations
entries.snippet: They gathered information using a "forest of antennas" installed on top of diplomatic buildings, the foreign minister says.

entries.title: Robots move in as waste firms struggle to find staff
entries.snippet: Humanoid robots are being added to the automation of waste sorting.

entries.title: Mali junta leader names himself defence minister after predecessor killed
entries.snippet: Former Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in a massive offensive by combined jihadist and separatist forces.

entries.title: Spain seizes record amount of cocaine in Atlantic Ocean, authorities say
entries.snippet: The Civil Guard found between 30,000 and 45,000kg of the drug on a freighter headed to Libya.

entries.title: Modi's BJP conquers Bengal, one of India's toughest political frontiers
entries.snippet: West Bengal’s verdict could reshape not just the state’s politics, but the future trajectory of Modi’s BJP.

entries.title: Antisemitism 'allowed to come into the open' says Bondi victim's daughter
entries.snippet: Sheina Gutnick was the first person to give evidence at the royal commission investigating the attack.

entries.title: GameStop makes $55.5bn takeover offer for eBay
entries.snippet: GameStop's boss Ryan Cohen says he sees potential to make eBay a much bigger rival to Amazon.

entries.title: New alliance shakes up Nigerian political landscape
entries.snippet: Peter Obi and Rabiu Kwankwaso, who came third and fourth respectively in the last elections, switch parties.

entries.title: Three dead after monster truck crashes into crowd
entries.snippet: At least 38 people were injured when the vehicle ploughed into the crowd at a show in Colombia.

entries.title: European leaders converge on Armenia as Russia looks on
entries.snippet: Two European summits are being held in a country long considered Russia's closest ally in the region.

entries.title: North Korean side to make rare trip to South Korea
entries.snippet: A team of North Korean footballers will make a rare visit to South Korea later this month.

entries.title: Amsterdam bans public adverts for meat and fossil fuels
entries.snippet: Local politicians say the move is in line with the Dutch capital's environmental targets.

entries.title: Police officer lowered into crocodile-infested river to recover human remains
entries.snippet: Police suspect the human remains belong to a businessman swept away by floodwater last week.

entries.title: The looks turning heads on the Met Gala carpet
entries.snippet: The theme for 2026 Met Gala fundraiser is "Costume Art", exploring fashion as an embodied art form.

entries.title: See the looks from the 2026 Met Gala red carpet
entries.snippet: Extravagantly dressed celebrities and designers made their way up the famous Met staircase Monday evening. The fundraiser is one of the fashion industry's biggest nights.

entries.title: Hotels have a big World Cup problem: Bookings are running far below projections
entries.snippet: Nearly 80% of hotels surveyed by a body representing the industry reported bookings were running below projections — a worrisome sign with only weeks to go before the World Cup.

entries.title: Modi's party takes control of India's West Bengal in key state election
entries.snippet: The outcome is expected to strengthen Modi's political position midway through his third term.

entries.title: Here are the 2026 Pulitzer Prize winners
entries.snippet: Award winners include authors Daniel Kraus, Jill Lepore and Yiyun Li, opinion writer M. Gessen and staffers and contributors at The Washington Post , Reuters and AP.

entries.title: A United jet struck a light pole and a truck near Newark airport, police say
entries.snippet: New Jersey State Police say a United Airlines passenger jet struck a light pole and damaged a truck as it was coming in for a landing at Newark Liberty International Airport on Sunday.

entries.title: Supreme Court gives abortion pill mifepristone a 1-week reprieve from a major change
entries.snippet: The Supreme Court says rules that allow patients to get the abortion pill mifepristone through the mail can stay the same for at least a week.

entries.title: The U.S. fights to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as UAE says it's attacked by Iran
entries.snippet: The U.S. military said it battled Iranian forces and sank six small boats as it moved to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, while the United Arab Emirates said Iran fired missiles and drones its way.

entries.title: The many ways Trump wants to change D.C., from buildings to statues to parks
entries.snippet: Many of the changes Trump wants to make to the White House and the city are facing legal challenges. Some are reversible, while others could reshape the nation's capital for generations to come.

entries.title: Trump says U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships through the Strait. And, key Senate races to watch
entries.snippet: President Trump announced that the U.S will "guide" stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz. And, Democrats face an uphill battle to gain Senate control. Here are the key races to watch.

entries.title: Not sure what to get Mom for Mother's Day? Get ideas from our listeners
entries.snippet: Mother's Day is Sunday, May 10. If you're stumped on what to get the moms in your life, get some inspiration from our audience. They share the best gifts they've ever received from their loved ones.

entries.title: Driver plows car into people in shopping area in Germany, killing 2
entries.snippet: An additional three people were seriously injured in what officials believe was a deliberate rampage, Leipzig Mayor Burkhard Jung said.

entries.title: 2nd sloth transferred from troubled Sloth World has died, Central Florida Zoo says
entries.snippet: A second sloth taken to a central Florida zoo for rehabilitation has died out of a group given up by a yet-to-open tourist attraction facing scrutiny for the deaths of more than 30 other sloths imported from Guyana and Peru.

entries.title: U.S. and Iran truce tested over the Strait of Hormuz, threatening to reignite conflict
entries.snippet: The tenuous truce between the U.S. and Iran was tested Monday, as American ships clearing a lane in the Strait of Hormuz came under fire and sank Iranian boats. Iran also fired drones and missiles at a key oil terminal in the United Arab Emirates, spooking markets and raising con

entries.title: Analyzing the U.S. effort to reopen the Strait of Hormuz amid tensions
entries.snippet: The U.S. military remains poised to resume the war as tensions rose on Monday with President Trump threatening that if Iran fires on U.S. vessels, "they will be blown off the face of the earth." Ian Ralby, president of Auxilium Worldwide, and Ret. Admiral Andrew Loiselle, who has

entries.title: News Wrap: Rudy Giuliani recovers from pneumonia at a Florida hospital
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Monday, former New York city mayor Rudy Giuliani is recovering from pneumonia at a Florida hospital, three people died from a suspected outbreak of hantavirus on a cruise ship, authorities search for suspects in a mass shooting that left at least 23 people injure

entries.title: Secret Service agent fires on man with a gun close to the White House
entries.snippet: For the second time in as many weeks, a U.S. Secret Service agent fired a gun at an armed suspect in Washington D.C., less than a mile from the White House. The agency said an agent returned fire at a man carrying a gun. A bystander, a juvenile, was also injured. White House corr

entries.title: Why the Trump administration is paying nearly $2 billion to abandon wind farms
entries.snippet: Lawmakers from both parties are raising concerns about the Trump administration's spending decisions. In the latest example, the administration said it will pay nearly $1 billion to energy companies to abandon plans to build two wind farms off the U.S. coast. Liz Landers joins Am

entries.title: What the Supreme Court ruling means for abortion access and what comes next
entries.snippet: The Supreme Court on Monday temporarily allowed continued nationwide access to abortion medication that's often distributed by mail. The court issued a one-week stay on a lower court's ruling that would have led to sweeping changes in how Mifepristone, one of the two drugs common

entries.title: How the collapse of Spirit Airlines is impacting travelers and the wider aviation industry
entries.snippet: Spirit Airlines has officially shut down. The low-cost airline went out of business this weekend after 34 years. It's left members of the flying public in a tricky bind and the larger industry with some serious questions. Stephanie Sy has our look at how we got here and what come

entries.title: U.S. Forest Service cuts raise concerns on protecting public lands and fighting wildfires
entries.snippet: Sweeping changes are coming for the U.S. Forest Service, which manages roughly a third of America's public land. The agency announced a dramatic overhaul of cuts, closures and consolidation. That's on top of the Trump administration's latest budget request that seeks to slash bil

entries.title: Former federal workers speak out about being fired and why they're now running for office
entries.snippet: More than three dozen former federal workers who quit or lost their jobs last year, in the wake of cuts from the Department of Government Efficiency, or DOGE, are now running for political office. Most, but not all, are Democrats who say the assault on public service led them to

entries.title: Tamara Keith and Amy Walter on redrawing congressional maps and testing Trump's power
entries.snippet: NPR's Tamara Keith and Amy Walter of the Cook Political Report with Amy Walter join Amna Nawaz to discuss the latest political news, including the 2026 elections, the Supreme Court's abortion pill ruling and the push by some states to redraw their congressional maps.

entries.title: How to watch the 'Costume Art' Met Gala red carpet
entries.snippet: Didn't snag one of the pricey tickets or a spot on the ultra-exclusive guest list? The red carpet spectacle is available for all to watch online.

entries.title: Astronomers believe they've detected an atmosphere around a tiny, icy world beyond Pluto
entries.snippet: Just 300 miles or so across, this mini Pluto is thought to be the solar system's smallest object.

entries.title: Daniel Kraus' 'Angel Down' and Bess Wohl's 'Liberation' are among Pulitzer winners in the arts
entries.snippet: Pulitzer Prize officials awarded the fiction prize to an author with a long history in fantasy, horror and young adult novels.

entries.title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni settle lawsuit over 'It Ends With Us' dispute
entries.snippet: The two sides settled their legal dispute Monday ahead of a planned trial over Lively's claims that Baldoni conspired with publicists to preemptively destroy her reputation after she privately accused him of sexually harassing her on the movie set.

entries.title: Washington Post, AP, Reuters and Minnesota Star Tribune among Pulitzer winners for 2025 work
entries.snippet: The Post's coverage illuminated the fast-moving, sometimes opaque particulars of President Donald Trump's drive to reshape the national government, and judges credited the Post with detailing what the changes meant for individual Americans.

entries.title: Officers shot man who opened fire near Washington Monument, Secret Service says
entries.snippet: A man spotted carrying a gun in the vicinity of the White House by plainclothes officers and agents was shot by law enforcement Monday after he opened fire on them near the Washington Monument, the Secret Service said.

entries.title: Fewer AAPI adults experienced hate incidents in past year but racism concerns linger, poll finds
entries.snippet: Fewer Asian American and Pacific Islander adults are reporting overt anti-Asian attacks than during the height of the COVID-19 pandemic, a new AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll finds, but many still worry about racial discrimination.

entries.title: Who is Howard Lutnick and what's his connection to Jeffrey Epstein?
entries.snippet: The extent of Lutnick's relationship with Epstein came under heavy scrutiny after he gave contradictory statements about his ties to the disgraced financier.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Person shot near Washington Monument, US Secret Service says
entries.snippet: The White House was briefly locked down on Monday after the US Secret Service said an individual was shot by law enforcement near the Washington Monument.

entries.title: Russia offers Ukraine May 8-9 ceasefire amid WWII anniversary, but threatens major reprisals in same breath
entries.snippet: President Putin has unilaterally declared a two-day truce amid commemorations of the end of World War II in Moscow. Ukraine's President Zelenskyy proposed his own truce starting a couple of days earlier.

entries.title: Leipzig: 2 killed after car drives into crowd
entries.snippet: The driver was a 33-year-old German national who has now been detained. The incident occurred on Grimmaische Street in the heart of Leipzig.

entries.title: Dubai's image as a financial hub faces its biggest test yet
entries.snippet: Amid the smoldering Iran war, wealthy elites are quietly shifting capital and other assets out of Dubai and heading to Singapore and Switzerland. Just how resilient is the emirate's safe-haven reputation?

entries.title: Pushback in Nigeria over ex-Boko Haram fighter reintegration
entries.snippet: Nigeria is set to reintegrate more than 700 former Boko Haram fighters into society under a government deradicalization program. Authorities say this is key to ending the insurgency — but communities are skeptical.

entries.title: Why Saudi Arabia is withdrawing from sport
entries.snippet: There are few major sports left that are not supported by investment from Saudi Arabia. But with the kingdom pulling out of golf and canceling planned events and contracts elsewhere, is the Saudi sports era over?

entries.title: Nigeria evacuating 130 citizens from South Africa after anti-migrant protests turn violent
entries.snippet: As reports of xenophobic attacks in South Africa continue, Nigeria said it would repatriate its citizens who want to leave.

entries.title: How child soldiers in Sudan become influencers on TikTok
entries.snippet: Some Sudanese child soldiers have social media accounts are celebrated by millions of followers. Psychologists and other experts warn this is a dangerous trend.

entries.title: India news: Modi claims victory in West Bengal election
entries.snippet: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's Hindu nationalist BJP party is poised to govern the state for the first time ever. Modi hopes to make inroads into other opposition strongholds too.

entries.title: Decoding China: Beijing courts Europe amid US dysfunction
entries.snippet: China is hoping to secure new allies for a future multipolar world order as the partnership between the US and the European powers slides into a crisis.

entries.title: North Korea ramps up executions over foreign media, says NGO
entries.snippet: Many children of North Korean elites are "addicted to smuggled South Korean pop culture and American action movies," an expert told DW. Under Kim Jong Un, watching this content could put one's life at risk.

entries.title: Georg Baselitz, artist renowned for his upside-down paintings, dies
entries.snippet: He was thrown out of art school at the age of 18 because of his love for Picasso. The German artist who never lost his rebellious streak has died at the age of 88.

entries.title: EPC summit: Europe has 'gotten the message' from Trump
entries.snippet: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said at the opening of the EPC summit in Armenia that Europe has "heard" Washington's frustration over its conflict with Iran and are "stepping up."

entries.title: Angine de Poitrine: A masked rock band conquers the internet
entries.snippet: From underground experiment to sold-out international concerts within days — as the French-Canadian band Angine de Poitrine starts touring the world, we take a look at the viral phenomenon.

entries.title: Germany news: Merz plays down US military withdrawal plans
entries.snippet: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has said a US threat to pull troops out of Germany is "nothing new." Meanwhile, Germany’s interior minister says border controls will stay despite falling asylum numbers. DW has more.

entries.title: Women's Champions League: Bayern fall to Barcelona in semis
entries.snippet: Bayern Munich have come up short in their bid to win the Women's Champions League for the first time after losing to Barcelona in the semifinals.

entries.title: US transfers crew of seized Iranian ship to Pakistan
entries.snippet: Pakistan's Foreign Ministry said that the "confidence-building measure" was supported by both sides.

entries.title: North Korea women's soccer club to make rare South Korea visit
entries.snippet: A rare inter-Korean sports clash will take place in South Korea this month. For the first time in 8 years, a North Korean sports team will play in the South.

entries.title: Ex-New York Mayor Giuliani in hospital, condition 'critical'
entries.snippet: Rudy Giuliani was dubbed "America’s mayor" for leading New York City through the aftermath of the 9/11 terror attacks in 2001. But his reputation took a deep dive amid his support for Trump in recent years.
